channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Revlon Fails to Ensure Some Products Are Safe, FDA Warns
item.description: Revlon Group Holdings LLC didn’t properly test products for cancer-causing chemicals or mold, U.S. The agency asked for “a comprehensive assessment and remediation plan” to ensure Revlon’s quality unit can effectively function. Revlon and several other beauty product makers faced allegations from consumers in recent years linking hair relaxers to some types of cancer.

item.title: Eight Indicted for Impersonating Shipping Carriers in Multi-State Retail Theft Ring
item.description: Eight people were indicted for conspiring to impersonate shipping carriers in a wide-ranging retail theft ring throughout the U.S. The charges allege the defendants used fraudulent shipment information of real shipping carriers and brokers, impersonated legitimate companies and stole nearly $5 million in goods from logistics sites in Pennsylvania, Virginia and New Jersey. Charging documents show the defendants operated ring from October 2025 to April 2026, allegedly committing six thefts and stealing: $165,000 worth of lamb; $432,000 worth of cheese; $295,000 in beef; more than $266,000 in copper; and more than $3.3 million in cigarettes.

item.title: Musk’s xAI, Spacex Hit With Class Action Over Data Center ‘Nuisance’
item.description: Elon Musk’s xAI and SpaceX have been sued by Mississippi residents who say a power plant fueling nearby data centers is blasting “omnipresent and inescapable” noise that has eroded their health and home values. The lawsuit, made public on Tuesday in federal court in Oxford, Mississippi, claims Musk’s companies negligently failed to curb the disturbance and created a public nuisance through excessive and offensive noise. Related: British Lawmaker Sues Musk’s xAI Over Sexualized Grok Images “The artificial intelligence (AI) boom is wreaking havoc on communities across the United States” by subjecting thousands of residents to near-constant noise and vibrations, the lawsuit said.

item.title: The Readiness is All: New AIG CEO Andersen Talks Industry Relevance
item.description: Eric Andersen doesn’t have any more insight into what the next five years will look like than his peers, but the message he delivered to them about the industry’s role in a changing world is clear: The readiness is all. Our ability to help our clients, across industries and across the globe, to have confidence” to make investments that lead to innovation “requires that our company is ready to be with them in that process.” To Andersen, a former broker leader, insurer readiness means having industry expertise, having technical expertise, having global reach and being in a strong financial position to be able to take risks with customers. After discussing the evolving role of brokers, insurers, MGAs and insurance linked-securities market participants, Wilkinson specifically asked Andersen to describe the biggest changes he sees ahead for the industry over the next few years.

item.title: One in Five Insurers Is Deploying AI While Cutting the Training Budgets to Make It Work, New Survey Finds
item.description: “They’re staying optimistic while absorbing real pressure, deploying AI while cutting the training budgets that make it work, and sitting on operational intelligence they know is valuable but haven’t yet built the infrastructure to act on. But 20% are simultaneously cutting training budgets while only 7% are actively protecting them. And a growing number are deploying AI faster than they’re preparing the people expected to work alongside it.

item.title: Philippines quake to weigh on reinsurers despite insured losses capped by low penetration: AM Best
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: William Zhao rejoins Marsh as senior M&A adviser

item.title: PartnerRe APAC CEO James Beedle to retire after 36-year career
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FSB consults on sound practices for the responsible adoption of artificial intelligence (AI)
item.description: Press enquiries: +41 61 280 8477 [email protected] Ref: 9/2026 The Financial Stability Board (FSB) today published a consultation report on Sound Practices for Responsible Adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI) . The sound practices are not intended to establish an international standard, to impose a prescriptive approach for responsible AI adoption by financial institutions, nor to influence business decisions in adopting a certain AI technology. The sound practices cover: The FSB strongly encourages the board and senior management of financial institutions to reference this toolkit as they consider business strategy, technology adoption, and risk management in an increasingly AI enabled environment.

item.title: Sound Practices for Responsible Adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI): Consultation Report
item.description: To facilitate responsible AI adoption by financial institutions, the report proposes a menu of 12 sound practices that financial institutions could apply in their organisation-wide AI governance and management of the relevant stages of AI development and deployment (AI lifecycle). The sound practices aim to help the board and senior management of financial institutions as they consider business strategy, technology adoption, and risk management in an increasingly AI-enabled environment. Responsible AI adoption allows financial institutions to harness opportunities and benefits while minimising associated risks.

item.title: Massachusetts MPIUA gets $150m Mayflower Re 2026 cat bond priced below guidance
item.description: In our first update on the deal we reported that while the target size for this new Mayflower Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond remained unchanged at $150 million, the MPIUA was looking to capitalise on strong price execution in the cat bond market, with the price guidance lowered for both of the tranches of notes on offer. (Series 2026-1) catastrophe bond, with both tranches of notes pricing below their initial guidance ranges, sources have told Artemis. The Massachusetts Property Insurance Underwriting Association (MPIUA) has now successfully secured its targeted $150 million of multi-peril aggregate reinsurance protection through the Mayflower Re Ltd.

item.title: Mexico doubles parametric catastrophe insurance to ~$575m at 2026 renewal
item.description: The government of Mexico has now finalised its main insurance protection for natural disasters and severe weather events for the coming year, doubling the size of its parametric catastrophe insurance arrangement to around US $575 million for 2026 into 2027. The government said this “reinforces its commitment to protecting the population from potential natural disasters,” while the doubling of the coverage expands its “capacity to protect public assets and provide timely assistance to the population affected by natural phenomena.” The largely parametric catastrophe insurance renewal runs from June 5th 2026 to May 5th 2027 and provides risk transfer protection against earthquakes, volcanic eruptions, hurricanes, and floods of medium to high severity. Mexico has been utilising parametric risk transfer in both insurance and catastrophe bond formats for many years, as the country has learned to appreciate the benefit of predictable and responsive, fast paying protection against natural disaster events.

item.title: Gallagher expands Middle East operations with new strategic investment
item.description: Gallagher Re’s Nadim Semaan commented, “This investment in Saudi Arabia marks a significant new milestone in Gallagher’s journey across the Middle East. Gallagher explained that this development builds on its existing business in the Middle East and will enable the firm to service reinsurance clients from its Al Khobar office, with plans to open an office in Riyadh in the near future. Consolidating our specialty and reinsurance operations enables us to move forward with a unified vision, enhanced capabilities, and a platform designed for long-term growth across the Middle East.”

item.title: Steadfast gets A$7.7bn acquisition offer from Amwins and Dragoneer
item.description: Australian insurance broker Steadfast Group has received a conditional, non-binding and indicative offer from Amwins Group and Dragoneer Investment Group to acquire 100% of its outstanding share capital for A$6.00 per share in cash, valuing the company at approximately A$7.7 billion. Amwins and Dragoneer – known as the consortium – intend to act jointly to pursue the transaction, with Dragoneer acquiring ownership of Steadfast’s retail brokerage business and Amwins acquiring the Australian broker’s underwriting agency business. The offer represents a 51.9% premium over Steadfast’s A$3.95 closing price on 9 June 2026.

item.title: Angus Kench joins Munich Re Specialty, Global Markets, Australia
item.description: Global reinsurer Munich Re has appointed Angus Kench as Head of Commercial Claims at Munich Re Specialty – Global Markets, Australia, effective July 1st, 2026. Chris Mackinnon, Head of Munich Re Specialty – Global Markets, Australia, commented on the appointment, “Claims delivery is the ultimate value proposition of our business, and we are delighted to welcome Angus into this crucial role.” Most recently, he served on the Global Casualty Product Board of Liberty Specialty Markets (LSM) Asia Pacific, a division of Liberty Mutual Group.

item.title: Britain’s Garden Habits Are Making Their Homes Harder to Insure
item.description: The trend — often a quick fix to create space to park cars or to make garden maintenance easier — is emerging as a major factor in predicting how badly floods will affect homes. To drive home its point, Flood Re last month sponsored a garden at the Chelsea Flower Show, which is the absolute high-point of Britain’s horticultural calendar and regularly attended by Britain’s royals. The conventional wisdom is that “lower maintenance is better,” says Kelly Ostler-Coyle, director of corporate affairs at Flood Re, which has served as a backstop for Britain’s commercial insurers since 2016.

item.title: US, Iran Attack Each Other Over Apache Helicopter Downed Near Hormuz Strait
item.description: The US and Iran exchanged strikes overnight after President Donald Trump retaliated against Tehran for shooting down an American Apache helicopter.The skirmishes further jolted a two-month-old truce and exposed the fragility of talks between the warring sides aimed at securing a lasting peace in the Middle East. The US military said it had completed an operation that saw fighter jets strike Iranian air defenses, ground control stations, and radar sites near the Strait of Hormuz. While indirect talks between Iran and the US about an interim deal — meant to extend the ceasefire by two months and reopen the Strait of Hormuz — continue, the confrontations underscored how high tensions are running.

item.title: UK Police Call for Calm After Brutal Knife Attack in Belfast
item.description: UK authorities pleaded for calm following a knife attack in Belfast that led to the arrest of a Sudanese national and prompted protest calls from the far right. With the victim hospitalized in serious condition, Prime Minister Keir Starmer condemned the incident as “horrific” and “abhorrent.” The horrific attack in Belfast last night is sickening. Read more: War Overtakes Civil Unrest as Top Political Violence Exposure for Global Businesses “All of us have a responsibility now to urge calm and let the police do their job,” Northern Ireland Secretary Hilary Benn told the House of Commons on Tuesday.

item.title: Sun Life Asia resilience index shows financial security weakening as inflation pressures households
item.description: The post Sun Life Asia resilience index shows financial security weakening as inflation pressures households appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Sun Life has warned that rising living costs are eroding household financial resilience across Asia, as inflation continues to reshape spending habits and weaken long-term financial security. The findings come from the insurer’s third Financial Resilience Index, published by Sun Life Asia, which shows that only 25% of people surveyed are now considered highly financially resilient, down from 32% in 2025.

item.title: Kayna joins Guidewire InsurTech Vanguards as embedded insurance platform targets SME market
item.description: The post Kayna joins Guidewire InsurTech Vanguards as embedded insurance platform targets SME market appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Irish insurtech Kayna has joined the Guidewire InsurTech Vanguards programme, strengthening its position in the embedded insurance market as carriers increasingly seek new ways to serve small and medium-sized businesses (SMEs). We look forward to connecting Kayna with the Guidewire community as they help carriers reach this market more efficiently and serve it more meaningfully.” The move reflects broader momentum in embedded insurance, where insurers are increasingly partnering with software providers to distribute protection products at the point of customer interaction.

item.title: Risks are not new, but data centre demand to test Asian insurers’ mettle as capacity tightens
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Helia Group taps Mark Senkevics as CEO, managing director

item.title: Steadfast receives takeover offer
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Peel Mutual Selects Guidewire Cloud
item.description: (Image credit: EndreF/Pixabay.) Peel Mutual Insurance Company (Mississauga, Ontario) has selected Guidewire (San Mateo, Calif.) Cloud to continue modernizing its core insurance processes and support automation, data analytics and growth across its business. Peel Mutual, a member of the Ontario Mutual Insurance Association, will deploy Guidewire Cloud across all lines of business. “Moving to Guidewire Cloud is a significant step in Peel Mutual’s long-term strategy, enabling us to accelerate innovation and unlock new opportunities,” comments Matt LeMaire, COO, Peel Mutual.

item.title: Zinnia Launches Headless Annuity Order Entry
item.description: (Image credit: Zinnia) Zinnia (Greenwich, Conn.) has launched headless annuity order entry capabilities designed to let carriers, banks, broker-dealers and IMOs integrate directly with Zinnia’s platform while building their own digital front-end experiences. Zinnia says the headless order entry capabilities are built on a modular, service-based architecture that exposes core functions as composable APIs. The company says the Insured Retirement Institute recently approved four in-force transaction APIs built on the Enterprise API framework contributed by Zinnia, establishing a shared foundation for real-time, interoperable annuity servicing across carriers and distributors.

item.title: Stellantis Tells Owners of 1.3M Jeeps to Park Outside Over Fire Concerns
item.description: Stellantis is recalling more than 1.3 million Jeep SUVs and trucks worldwide over fire concerns and is urging owners to park outside and away from structures or other vehicles until a fix is completed. Related: Ford Recalling 420,000 US Vehicles Over Seat-Belt Issue NHTSA opened an investigation in September 2024 into nearly 800,000 Jeep Wrangler and Gladiator vehicles from the 2021-2023 model years over engine fire concerns, saying the hydraulic power steering pump wiring may overheat and cause a vehicle fire, even when the vehicle is parked with the ignition in the “Off” position. In a filing with the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration on Tuesday, Stellantis said the recall covers 2021 through 2025 model year Jeep Wrangler SUVs and Jeep Gladiator trucks over an electrical connection issue in the electric hydraulic power steering pump wiring.

item.title: Seat Belt Issue Prompts Ford Recall, Plus a Do-not-drive Order for Some Broncos, Mavericks
item.description: Ford is recalling almost 420,000 vehicles because of a seat belt issue that may result in an injury if a crash occurs. They can take their vehicle to a Ford or Lincoln dealer to have both front seat belt retractors inspected and to replace retractors involved in the recall for free. Individuals may also face injury in some instances if the seat belt retracts rapidly.

item.title: World Cup Could Cost Employers as Workers Tune into Matches: Survey
item.description: Research from UKG estimates that the World Cup, which runs from June 11 to July 19, could cost global employers some $17 billion in lost productivity, with 37% of workers planning to adjust their schedule because of the tournament. As the soccer World Cup is set to take over water cooler chats, employers may have trouble keeping workers focused during the tournament – and even getting them to the office in the first place, a new survey published last week suggests. The survey found 27% of employees are likely to miss work by showing up late, leaving early or skipping entirely, while 11% admitted they would work while hungover and 14% said they would secretly stream matches and highlights while on the clock.

item.title: IIHS Reveals Driver Protection Ratings for 9 Commercial Vehicles
item.description: The driver protection assessment focuses on airbags, seat belt force limiters and pretensioners, and belt reminder systems because they not always included in commercial vehicles. Four of the first nine heavy-duty pickups and cargo vans to be assessed come with a suite of features that IIHS considers essential for driver protection — including standard front and side airbags, advanced seat belt technology and effective seat belt reminders. regulators do not currently require automatic emergency braking (AEB) or other advanced driver assistance systems for commercial vehicles, especially important for heavy trucks and vans, whose large size makes them a greater danger to other road users.

item.title: Saudi Re taps Ruchi Jain to lead Gift City operations
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: IAG warns New Zealand’s ‘piecemeal’ approach to natural hazards is unsustainable
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FSB regulatory and supervisory modernisation symposium
item.description: The Financial Stability Board (FSB) hosted a Modernisation Symposium on 9 June 2026 in Basel, bringing together representatives of authorities, academia, and industry to discuss methods and challenges of regulatory and supervisory modernisation. Key discussions during the symposium focused on the drivers of change in regulatory and supervisory frameworks, the use of cost-benefit analysis, and tailoring/proportionality in regulatory and supervisory processes. The event was part of the FSB’s work to promote well-aligned modernisation efforts and support members in making regulation and supervision more effective.

item.title: Icosa Investments confirms Dell’Amore hire as Partner to expand institutional offering
item.description: Icosa has now confirmed Dell’Amore’s appointment as a Partner at the company, with his official start set for July 2026. Raffaele Dell’Amore as a Partner and member of the firm’s management team. Florian Steiger served as Head of Cat Bonds, while Raffaele Dell’Amore was responsible for the Private ILS Strategies,” Icosa said.

item.title: NJM Insurance targets its largest cat bond yet, with now $250m Lower Ferry Re 2026-1
item.description: NJM Insurance has set its sights on sponsoring its largest catastrophe bond so far, with all tranches of notes now having target sizes that overall seek to secure $250 million of northeast US named storm reinsurance from the capital markets from its new Lower Ferry Re Ltd. The Lower Ferry Re 2023-1 cat bond is scheduled to mature this June, so it now appears that NJM Insurance is set to more than replace the reinsurance protection that expiring cat bond had provided it with. Series 2026-1 cat bond sponsorship, three tranches of notes were offered but at first only two were sized, giving an initial target from NJM Insurance of securing $150 million or more in reinsurance protection.

item.title: Cyber, AI and economic pressures dominate insurance concerns in RiskScan 2026
item.description: According to RiskScan 2026, a cross-market research study by the Insurance Information Institute (Triple-I) and Munich Re US, cyber incidents, economic pressures, and artificial intelligence have emerged as the top concerns across five key insurance market segments, underscoring the growing interconnectedness of modern risk. insurance markets, with RiskScan 2026 highlighting a broad convergence of cyber, economic, technological, and catastrophe risks. RiskScan 2026 observed that today’s digital landscape features an increasing frequency and complexity of cyber threats, from companies vulnerable to customer data breaches to smart homes that are interconnected and exposed to potential attacks.

item.title: MSIG USA receives AM Best A+ rating affirmations and new rating for E&S insurer
item.description: MSIG USA, the US insurance arm of global insurer MS&AD Insurance Group Holdings, has announced that its newly launched excess and surplus (E&S) lines carrier, MSIG Specialty Insurance America, Inc., has been assigned an A+ (Superior) Financial Strength Rating and an “aa” (Superior) Long-Term Issuer Credit Rating by AM Best. Alongside the new rating assignment, AM Best reaffirmed the A+ (Superior) Financial Strength Ratings and “aa” (Superior) Long-Term Issuer Credit Ratings of Mitsui Sumitomo Insurance Company of America, Mitsui Sumitomo Insurance USA, Inc., and MSIG Specialty Insurance USA, Inc. According to MSIG USA, the new rating marks an important milestone for MSIG Specialty Insurance America, Inc., which was established to support the company’s expanding E&S operations in the United States.

item.title: ICEYE targets global expansion as latest funding round surpasses €1bn
item.description: Proceeds from this funding round will be used to expand ICEYE’s global footprint and deepen its intelligence capabilities. ICEYE, a provider of sovereign intelligence from space, has raised €450 million (USD 520 million) in a primary Series F funding round led by General Atlantic, at a valuation of more than €10 billion (USD 12 billion). Combined with a secondary placement, the total raised from this funding round exceeded €1 billion (USD 1.2 billion).

item.title: Texas Governor Vows Sped Up Construction of Screwworm Fly Breeding Facility
item.description: Department of Agriculture accelerate construction of the $750 million breeding facility outside Edinburg, Texas, about 20 miles (32 kilometers) north of the U.S.-Mexico border. He said Texas is willing to spend its own funds to see that construction is “24 hours a day, seven days a week.” Without greater sterile fly production, Abbott said during a news conference in the state capital of Austin, “We cannot make it through a second summer.” The USDA confirmed an infestation of New World screwworm fly larvae this week in a 3-week-old calf in La Pryor, Texas, about 100 miles (161 kilometers) southwest of San Antonio and 50 miles (80 kilometers) from the Mexico border. Greg Abbott expressed concern last week that a new factory isn’t expected to start breeding sterile New World screwworm flies for more than a year as a big part of the effort to stop its flesh-eating larvae from threatening the $113 U.S.

item.title: Israel Kills Eight in Strike on Lebanon; US Rescues Helicopter Crew From Hormuz
item.description: Israel struck the historic port city of Tyre in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, killing at least eight people, in an escalation that adds strain to efforts to broker a peace deal to end the wider Middle East war. The eight victims were killed in a single strike on the city’s eastern edge, Lebanon’s health ministry said. President Donald Trump to end their first direct exchanges of fire since April, but Tehran warned it would resume hostilities if Israel continued to attack its ally Hezbollah in Lebanon.

item.title: Rio Grande Valley Farmers are Bracing for Severe Drought
item.description: While not in drought — yet — if predictions are correct, the Rio Grande Valley’s produce industry could be down a total of 40% of their typical fruit and vegetable plantings due to drier weather later this summer. For much of the year, farmers and ranchers were preparing for a tough summer of drought. As of Monday, nearly 50% of the state was experiencing some level of drought conditions according to the U.S.

item.title: NFP acquires assets of Signature Personal Insurance
item.description: NFP , an Aon company, has acquired certain assets of Signature Personal Insurance (SPI), a Kansas City-based independent personal lines agency focused on high-net-worth clients. Their high-net-worth personal property and casualty insurance tools will strengthen our ability to deliver integrated risk management solutions to individuals and families while also expanding our presence in the Kansas City market.” – Brett Woodward, president of Personal Risk at NFP. As part of the transaction, SPI founder Roper DeGarmo will join NFP as vice president, reporting to Mary Mullen, senior vice president of Personal Risk in NFP’s Central Region.

item.title: Sedgwick launches AI-powered claims ecosystem
item.description: Sedgwick has introduced Omni, a proprietary digital ecosystem designed to support claims and risk management operations through the use of artificial intelligence, machine learning, and the company’s claims data. Omni combines Sedgwick’s AI tools with capabilities such as document and call summarization, digital triage, severity modeling, automated reserving, fraud detection, and quality oversight to improve consistency and efficiency across the claims lifecycle. “The AI landscape is changing fast every day and so is the claims environment, and Sedgwick has always been in lockstep with these evolutions.

item.title: Zinnia launches API-based annuity ordering capabilities
item.description: Zinnia has launched headless annuity order entry capabilities, allowing carriers, banks, broker-dealers, and independent marketing organizations to build and control their own digital experiences while connecting directly to Zinnia’s insurance infrastructure through APIs. The launch follows the recent approval by the Insured Retirement Institute of four in-force transaction APIs based on Zinnia’s Enterprise API framework, aimed at improving interoperability and real-time annuity servicing across carriers and distributors. According to Zinnia, the capability is built on its API-first architecture and is designed to support growing demand for customizable distribution experiences while reducing reliance on legacy systems and predefined interfaces.

item.title: BASF Warns Iran War Could Trigger Supply Chain Disruption for Carmakers
item.description: BASF SE Chief Executive Officer Markus Kamieth said rising inflation and the risk of supply-chain disruption from the U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran have darkened the outlook for both the auto sector and the broader economy. Shortages of materials are becoming more likely and could halt tightly run supply chains such as car production, Kamieth said at a gathering for journalists in Frankfurt. Related: Why Toyota RAV4s Are Suddenly the Most Coveted Used Cars in America BASF is a major supplier to the global auto industry, making coatings, plastics, catalysts, battery materials and other chemicals used by carmakers and their suppliers.

item.title: Extreme Weather to Drive $20 Trillion in Spending
item.description: Extreme weather will likely spur more than $20 trillion in global spending over the next decade, boosting sales and earnings for reinsurers and companies involved in energy efficiency and climate security, according to analysts at Bloomberg Intelligence. Costs from extreme-weather events totaled $1.4 trillion last year, equal to about 1.2% of global gross domestic product, the BI analysts said. Spending on climate adaptation has helped drive gains for companies including BWX Technologies Inc., RenaissanceRe Holdings Ltd., Woodward Inc.

item.title: State Bank of India confirms listing plans for general insurance arm: reports
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Chinese regulator fines Taiping’s mainland non-life unit over compliance lapses
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: AI Services And Robotics Lead Diverse Crop Of 29 New May Unicorns As SpaceX, Anthropic And OpenAI Line Up Blockbuster Exits
item.description: OpenAI and Anthropic each launched multibillion-dollar deployment ventures staffed with forward-deployed engineers, while a long list of startups building AI infrastructure, autonomous software and robotics also reached unicorn status. The Unicorn Board reached $9.9 trillion in value in May, as Anthropic moved ahead of OpenAI to become the second most valued private company after SpaceX . Beyond AI, new unicorns were minted across many sectors including healthcare, quantum, aerospace, financial services, manufacturing, e-commerce and energy.

item.title: Stubbings appointed Chairman of Miller Bermuda to drive reinsurance & retro growth
item.description: Miller, the independent specialist re/insurance broker has appointed Peter Stubbings, a renowned reinsurance and retrocession broking team leader as Chairman of Miller Bermuda, where he will support the continued growth of the firm’s reinsurance and retro offering across the Bermuda market. Drawing on his long-standing relationships with reinsurance clients, Miller noted that in his new role, Stubbings will support new business generation by facilitating cross-selling opportunities between AHJ Miller, Miller’s reinsurance division, and the wider Miller Bermuda business. We’re delighted to have Peter onboard.” Addressing his appointment, Stubbings said: “It’s a pleasure to be joining Miller at this exciting time of expansion for both our Bermuda business and our global reinsurance capabilities.

item.title: Artemis Bermuda ILS Executive Roundtable 2026
item.description: Welcome to Artemis’ 2026 Bermuda ILS Executive Roundtable , during which leaders and experts from across the insurance-linked securities sector discussed the momentum being seen in catastrophe bonds and ILS this year, as well as the continued importance of Bermuda’s role in the market. In partnership with Appleby and Moody’s , the Artemis Bermuda ILS Executive Roundtable 2026 report is now available to download for free . Artemis held its seventh Bermuda insurance-linked securities (ILS) executive roundtable in Hamilton in April, bringing together leaders from across the expanding sector to discuss current market trends and future opportunities.
